# Medical Case Record Sample

## Taiyang Wind Stroke Case

### Symptoms
Fever, sweating, aversion to wind, moderate pulse, nasal congestion, retching.

### Syndrome Differentiation
In Taiyang wind stroke, the yang is floating while the yin is weak. When yang floats, heat arises spontaneously; when yin is weak, sweat emerges on its own. For symptoms such as slight aversion to cold, mild aversion to wind, gentle fever, nasal congestion, and retching, Guizhi Decoction is the main treatment.

### Treatment Principle
Relieve the muscles to expel exterior pathogens, harmonize nutritive and defensive qi.

### Prescription
Guizhi Decoction: Cinnamon Twig 3 liang (peeled), Peony 3 liang, Licorice 2 liang (prepared), Ginger 3 liang (sliced), Jujube 12 pieces (broken).

### Decoction Method
Take the five ingredients, cut three of them, add 7 sheng of water, simmer over low heat to obtain 3 sheng, remove the dregs, adjust to suitable temperature, and take 1 sheng. Soon after taking, drink about 1 sheng of hot rice gruel to aid the medicinal effect, cover with a blanket for about one hour, and it is better to have a slight sweat all over the body; do not let the sweat flow profusely, otherwise the disease will not be cured.

### Case Analysis
This case is a typical Taiyang wind stroke syndrome. The patient experienced wind pathogen invasion, unconsolidated defensive qi, and leakage of nutritive yin, resulting in symptoms such as fever, sweating, and aversion to wind. Treatment with Guizhi Decoction to relieve the muscles, expel exterior pathogens, and harmonize nutritive and defensive qi, leading to pathogen elimination and recovery.

## Taiyang Cold Damage Case

### Symptoms
Headache, fever, body pain, low back pain, joint pain, aversion to wind, absence of sweating, and asthma.

### Syndrome Differentiation
In Taiyang disease, for symptoms such as headache, fever, body pain, low back pain, joint pain, aversion to wind, absence of sweating, and asthma, Mahuang Decoction is the main treatment.

### Treatment Principle
Induce sweating to expel exterior pathogens, ventilate the lungs to relieve asthma.

### Prescription
Mahuang Decoction: Ephedra 3 liang (de-jointed), Cinnamon Twig 2 liang (peeled), Licorice 1 liang (prepared), Apricot Kernel 70 pieces (peeled and tipped).

### Decoction Method
Take the four ingredients, add 9 sheng of water, first boil Ephedra to reduce by 2 sheng, remove the froth, add the other ingredients, boil to obtain 2.5 sheng, remove the dregs, warm and take 8 he, cover to induce slight sweating, no need to drink porridge, follow the same aftercare as Guizhi Decoction.

### Case Analysis
This case is a typical Taiyang cold damage syndrome. The patient experienced cold pathogen invasion, which constrained defensive yang and stagnated nutritive yin, resulting in symptoms such as headache, fever, body pain, and absence of sweating. Treatment with Mahuang Decoction to induce sweating, expel exterior pathogens, ventilate the lungs, and relieve asthma, allowing cold pathogens to be expelled with sweat.

## Shaoyang Disease Case

### Symptoms
Alternating chills and fever, fullness and discomfort in the chest and hypochondria, reluctance to talk and eat, vexation and propensity to vomit, bitter taste in the mouth, dry throat, and dizziness.

### Syndrome Differentiation
In cold damage or wind stroke of five to six days, for symptoms such as alternating chills and fever, fullness and discomfort in the chest and hypochondria, reluctance to talk and eat, vexation and propensity to vomit, or chest vexation without vomiting, or thirst, or abdominal pain, or hypochondriac fullness and hardness, or palpitations in the epigastrium and urinary difficulty, or absence of thirst with slight fever, or cough, Xiaochaihu Decoction is the main treatment.

### Treatment Principle
Harmonize Shaoyang.

### Prescription
Xiaochaihu Decoction: Bupleurum 8 liang, Scutellaria 3 liang, Ginseng 3 liang, Licorice 3 liang (prepared), Ginger 3 liang (sliced), Jujube 12 pieces (broken), Pinellia 0.5 sheng (washed).

### Decoction Method
Take the seven ingredients, add 12 sheng of water, boil to obtain 6 sheng, remove the dregs, simmer again to obtain 3 sheng, warm and take 1 sheng, three times a day.

### Case Analysis
This case is a typical Shaoyang disease syndrome. The patient experienced pathogen invasion of Shaoyang, which disrupted the pivot function, resulting in symptoms such as alternating chills and fever, fullness and discomfort in the chest and hypochondria, and vexation and propensity to vomit. Treatment with Xiaochaihu Decoction to harmonize Shaoyang, leading to pathogen elimination and recovery.
